response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and distributed-systems engineer whose career is built almost entirely on web technologies — TypeScript, Node.js, Ember.js, and DLang. The role demands expert-level C++ as its primary competency, yet the candidate's C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor's-thesis audio project. There is no evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, IoT development, MQTT or industrial-protocol experience, IEC 62443 security standards, or Azure cloud work, all of which are core or strongly advantageous requirements. The profile is a strong web-engineering match but a very poor fit for this role.
strengths: Demonstrated event-driven and distributed-systems design at scale (micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby).
Broad agile/craftsmanship culture including TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and mentoring — aligns with the behavioral competencies.
Node.js and REST API depth satisfies a small subset of the advantageous skills listed in the JD.
concerns: C++ experience is negligible relative to the role — approximately six months in 2014–2015 and one thesis project; expert-level C++ and STL mastery is the primary requirement.
Zero industrial-automation, IoT, or embedded-systems background; no exposure to Modbus, MQTT, OPC UA, IEC 61850, or similar industrial protocols.
No indication of secure-development lifecycle (SDL) knowledge or IEC 62443 industrial-cybersecurity standards, which are core security expectations for the position.
